title: Part 1: Multi-source BFS — layers are distances

cues.label: Seed the sources
cues.headline: Enqueue every rotten cell at 0
cues.narration: Before the loop, enqueue every already-rotten cell at minute zero and count the fresh cells.

cues.label: One layer per minute
cues.headline: Process the whole frontier
cues.narration: Each minute pops the entire current frontier; every fresh neighbor flips to rotten and joins the next layer.

cues.label: Layer = distance
cues.headline: Rot minute is nearest-source distance
cues.narration: Because all sources started together, the minute a cell flips is its distance to the nearest rotten cell.

cues.label: Mark on enqueue
cues.headline: Each cell enters once
cues.narration: Flipping a cell rotten the moment it is enqueued stops two neighbors from processing it twice.

cues.label: The answer
cues.headline: fresh == 0 at minute 4
cues.narration: Here fresh reaches zero at minute four, the deepest layer, so the answer is four minutes.

cues.label: Unreachable
cues.headline: Leftover fresh ⇒ -1
cues.narration: If any cell is still fresh when the queue empties, it can never rot, so the answer is negative one.
